name: Zoobe Junction Truck Bombing — 587+ Killed
detail: Deadliest attack in Somali history. Military-grade explosive truck bomb at busy intersection in central Mogadishu. October 14, 2017. Al-Shabaab did not immediately claim but is consensus responsible.

name: Kampala World Cup Bombings — 74 Killed
detail: Al-Shabaab's first external attack. Coordinated bombings at rugby club and Ethiopian Village restaurant in Kampala, Uganda, targeting crowds watching FIFA World Cup final.

name: Westgate Mall Siege, Nairobi — 67 Killed
detail: Four-day siege of Westgate Shopping Centre, Nairobi. Gunmen targeted non-Muslim shoppers. 67 killed including multiple Western nationals. Retaliation for KDF presence in Somalia.

name: Garissa University Massacre, Kenya — 148 Students Killed
detail: Al-Shabaab gunmen stormed Garissa University College, separating and executing Christian students. 148 killed in Kenya's worst terrorist attack since 1998. KDF presence in Somalia cited as motivation.

name: El Adde KDF Base Overrun — 140–180 Soldiers Killed
detail: Pre-dawn assault on Kenya's AMISOM forward operating base. Vehicle bombs breached perimeter; Al-Shabaab fighters overwhelmed the garrison. Kenya's worst military loss since independence. Al-Shabaab released video of the attack.

name: Manda Bay Airfield Attack — 3 Americans Killed
detail: Al-Shabaab assault on Kenya's Manda Bay Airfield used by US forces. Three US personnel killed, aircraft destroyed. Triggered major US airstrike retaliation campaign.

name: Mogadishu Education Ministry Attack — 21 Killed
detail: VBIED plus gunmen attacked the Ministry of Education during business hours. 21 killed, dozens wounded. Security forces secured building after several hours. Underscored Al-Shabaab's continued Mogadishu penetration.

name: Wave of Hotel Attacks, Mogadishu (2015–2016)
detail: Multiple Al-Shabaab VBIED and gunmen attacks on Mogadishu hotels: Sahafi Hotel (Nov 2015, 20+ killed), Central Hotel (Feb 2015, 25+ killed). Pattern of targeting venues hosting government meetings and internationals.

name: Jazeera Palace Hotel Attack, Mogadishu (2024)
detail: Al-Shabaab attack on upscale hotel frequently used by government officials and foreign visitors. VBIED at gate followed by armed assault. Security forces neutralized attackers after multi-hour siege.

name: Al-Shabaab Counter-Offensive in Hiiraan (2023)
detail: Following ATMIS Phase 1 drawdown in June 2023, Al-Shabaab launched coordinated counter-offensives in Hiiraan region, retaking several villages previously cleared by SNA/Macawisley forces. Ambushes on SNA convoys inflicted significant casualties.

name: Lido Beach Restaurant Attack — 37 Killed, 200+ Injured
detail: Al-Shabaab gunmen stormed a popular beachside restaurant on Lido Beach, Mogadishu, on August 2, 2024, killing 37 civilians and injuring over 200 during a busy Friday evening. Victims included families with children and young people. The attack targeted one of Mogadishu's most frequented public spaces, demonstrating Al-Shabaab's ability to strike in the capital beyond the Green Zone.

name: Oswein Triple Base Overrun — 150–178 SNA Killed
detail: Al-Shabaab overran three Somali National Army forward operating bases at Oswein, north of Mogadishu, in August 2023. An estimated 150-178 SNA fighters were killed—the group's most significant tactical success in years. The Somali government publicly denied the scale of losses. Al-Shabaab released captured vehicles and soldiers as propaganda, and used the victory to demonstrate ATMIS drawdown had left SNA exposed.
